user.title: Connect Emby server
user.description: Connect Home Assistant to your local Emby server. The API key is stored in Home Assistant and redacted from diagnostics.

reconfigure.title: Edit connection
reconfigure.description: Change the server address, port, HTTPS setting or API key. Leave the API-key field empty to keep the stored key.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the Emby server.

abort.already_configured: This Emby server is already configured.
abort.unique_id_mismatch: The new connection belongs to another Emby server.

init.title: Manage EMBi
init.description:
Manage Emby players in Home Assistant and safely clean up old device records when needed.

{server_history} devices in Emby server device history
{ha_players} players in Home Assistant
{protected} protected by active playback
{removable} currently removable from Home Assistant
Automatic cleanup: {automatic_cleanup}

devices_players.title: Devices & players
devices_players.description:
Choose which Emby players appear in Home Assistant.

{ha_players} players in Home Assistant
{playing} currently playing or paused
{known_users} known Emby users

Available groups:
{groups}

data.technical_access_visibility: Show technical access as Home Assistant players
data.search_query: Search user, device, app or entity ID

data_description.only_during_playback: Off: selected players remain available when nothing is playing. On: players appear only while playing or paused.
data_description.auto_show_new_players: When off, new playback devices remain hidden until you enable them here.
data_description.technical_access_visibility: Technical access means API applications that are not normal playback devices. Uncertain clients remain classified as unknown.
data_description.search_query: The search uses friendly user, device and app names plus the full Home Assistant entity ID.
data_description.selected_group: Known users are listed first, followed by shared devices, devices without user assignment, technical access and unknown clients.

player_group.title: Devices & players

data.show_user_players: Show all players for this user

data_description.show_user_players: Turning this off hides players assigned only to this user while preserving individual choices.
data_description.visible_player_keys: Playing and paused players are protected. Hidden players can be shown again later.
data_description.enable_entity_ids: A disabled but valid entity is not an orphan. Enabling preserves its identity and metadata.

cleanup.title: Cleanup

data.server_auto_cleanup_remove_ha_entities: Also remove matching Home Assistant players

data_description.server_auto_cleanup_enabled: EMBi regularly checks old records and removes only records that satisfy all safety rules.
data_description.server_auto_cleanup_remove_ha_entities: A related Home Assistant player is removed only when no remaining Emby record needs it and it is neither playing nor paused.
data_description.manual_age_preset: Manual server checks remain available when automatic cleanup is off.
data_description.cleanup_action: Destructive actions are available only while no normal draft changes are pending.

server_history_check.title: Check Emby server device history
server_history_check.description:
{total} records checked
{candidates} are older than {age_days} days and safe to delete
{active} are protected by active playback
{recent} do not satisfy the age rule
{without_activity} have no valid activity date

When no safe candidates exist, no empty selector is shown and nothing is changed.

confirm_server_deletion.title: Delete {count} Emby server records?
confirm_server_deletion.description: The selected device-history records will be removed from the Emby server. User accounts, media, libraries and playback history are not deleted. This is the single final confirmation.

manage_ha_players.title: Home Assistant players
manage_ha_players.description:
{ha_players} players in Home Assistant
{removable} currently removable
{protected} protected by playback or an unresolved state

All players:
{all_rows}

Protected players:
{protected_rows}

data.selected_ha_entity_ids: Players to remove from Home Assistant

data_description.selected_ha_entity_ids: Selected players are hidden in EMBi and removed from Home Assistant. Their Emby server-history records remain and they can be restored later under Devices & players.

confirm_ha_removal.title: Remove {count} players from Home Assistant?
confirm_ha_removal.description: The selected players will be hidden in EMBi and removed from Home Assistant. They remain in Emby server device history and can be restored later. Playing and paused players are rechecked and protected. This is the single final confirmation.

review_changes.title: Review changes
review_changes.description:
{count} semantic changes are pending. Nothing has been written yet.

{changes}

error.cannot_connect: Could not refresh current Emby and Home Assistant data.
error.playback_protected: Stop playback before hiding or removing this player.
error.invalid_age: Choose a preset or enter a valid custom number of days.
error.invalid_selection: The selected target changed or is no longer safe. Reopen the page and try again.
error.confirmation_required: Use the final confirmation to continue.

abort.cannot_connect: The action could not be completed safely because current data was unavailable.
abort.storage_failed: The persistent maintenance state could not be saved. No options were applied.
abort.unsaved_changes: Apply or discard normal draft changes before starting a destructive action.
abort.apply_complete: Changes applied. Removed: {removed}. Restored: {restored}. Enabled: {enabled}. Protected: {protected}. Failed: {failed}.
abort.ha_player_removal_complete: Home Assistant player removal completed. Requested: {requested}. Removed: {removed}. Protected: {protected}. Failed: {failed}.

options.manual_check: Check Emby server device history now
